approach_summary: Probed IntelliJ MCP with get_project_modules (159 modules, confirmed available). Used search_symbol to locate OrderCommonRuleService (found single class at we-business-order-audit/.../OrderCommonRuleService.java:11, Spring @Component). Used get_symbol_info on the class, supports() and execute() — returned signatures/docs but not a usages list in this MCP build. Fell back to search_in_files_by_text for 'OrderCommonRuleService' to enumerate references: found one subclass (OrderRuleService extends it) and only self-references/tags. Since the class is a @Component("OrderCommonRuleService") implementing SimpleRuleService, followed framework wiring: searched getSimpleRuleService to find the dispatch in DefaultRuleEngineService (getBean by name) and DefaultOrganizationAuditRuleService:109-111 (the lone invocation site keyed by DB-configured rule name). Confirmed the literal "OrderCommonRuleService" appears only at the @Component annotation, so there are no compile-time callers of execute/supports; runtime invocation is through Spring bean lookup driven by audit_organization_rules DB rows. The subclass OrderRuleService overrides execute without calling super and does not invoke supports.
